stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of PM2.5 concentration using an air quality sensor. The measurements have a mean of 65.8 µg/m³ and a standard deviation of 9.1 µg/m³. However, calibration tests reveal that an air quality sensor systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.7% and 6.1%. Estimate the true PM2.5 concentration and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 65.8 µg/m³. After correcting for the 3.9% bias: ≈ 63.3 µg/m³. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.7%-6.1%) and sampling variability: [58.7, 68.0]. Point estimate: 63.3 µg/m³.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
